title: Microcerasus incisa
taxonomy: Microcerasus incisa is classified as a species of shrub in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Microcerasus, which is a part of the subfamily Amygdaloideae. This genus is closely related to the genus Amygdalus, which includes the wild almond. Within the Rosaceae family, Microcerasus incisa is most closely related to other genera such as Cerasus and Prunus. The classification of Microcerasus incisa is still subject to ongoing research and potential revisions in the field of taxonomy.

intro: Microcerasus incisa, also known as the cut-leaved hawthorn or cut-leaf hawthorn, is a plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its thorny shrubs and small white flowers. The plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Microcerasus incisa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
